# Data Flows — Open edX

_Generated 2026-06-24 16:04 UTC · c4gen dev_

Each scenario below replays one interaction as a C4 **Dynamic** diagram.
Amber steps are asynchronous (queued / scheduled / event-driven).

/// admonition | How to read these diagrams
    type: info

These are [C4 model](https://c4model.com/) diagrams (C4-PlantUML). Read them
top-down: **System Context** (the whole SOA) → **Container** (one system's
runtime units) → **Dynamic** (a single data flow, step by step).

* **People** are rounded boxes; **systems** and **containers** are
  rectangles; **databases** and **queues** have distinct shapes.
* Each arrow is a data flow labelled with *what* moves.
* <span style="color:#4c9be8">**Solid arrows**</span> are
  **synchronous** (request/response, caller blocks).
* <span style="color:#e8a33d">**Amber dashed arrows**</span> are
  **asynchronous** (queued, scheduled, or event-driven — caller does not block).
* **Drag to pan, scroll to zoom.** Boxes with a link drill into the next level.
///

## Learner takes a course (synchronous)

A learner works through courseware in the LMS. The LMS loads course structure from the modulestore (MongoDB), reads/writes state in MySQL, proxies forum activity to the comments service, and stores annotations in the Notes API.

<div class="c4-box" data-c4-svg="../_diagrams/flow-take-course.svg"></div>

## Course authoring & publish (synchronous + async)

An author edits a course in Studio. Studio writes structure to the modulestore, stores uploaded assets and (on demand) course export archives to S3, and indexes content into edx-search.

<div class="c4-box" data-c4-svg="../_diagrams/flow-authoring.svg"></div>

## App enrollment, grade & certificate sync (cross-service, mixed)

MITx Online pushes user creation and enrollments to the LMS REST API and reads grades; the LMS posts enrollment/certificate webhooks back. A periodic MITx Online Celery task retries failed enrollments and repairs faulty edX users. xPRO and MicroMasters similarly pull grades/certificates on a batch.

<div class="c4-box" data-c4-svg="../_diagrams/flow-enroll-grade-sync.svg"></div>

## App SSO via Open edX (synchronous)

xPRO and MicroMasters authenticate learners against Open edX as the OAuth2 identity provider. The provider verifies the social-auth login, provisions an edX user + access token, and persists OAuth state in MySQL. On the mitxonline stack, Open edX itself federates login to Keycloak.

<div class="c4-box" data-c4-svg="../_diagrams/flow-sso.svg"></div>

## ODL video registration into edxval (cross-service, async)

ODL Video Service fetches a short-lived JWT and POSTs/PATCHes transcoded video metadata (HLS/MP4 CloudFront URLs) to the LMS edxval API so the videos play inside Open edX courses.

<div class="c4-box" data-c4-svg="../_diagrams/flow-video-register.svg"></div>

## Ingestion sources (ETL)

Every external source the `edx_content` / `default` Celery workers pull from, with transport and cadence. ⚠️ marks brittle linkages (HTML/token scrapes, hardcoded URLs).

| Source | Transport | Cadence | Data | Source of truth |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **MITx Online (enrollment/grade API consumer)** | REST OAuth2 | on enroll / scheduled | enrollments + current grades | `openedx/api.py` |
| **MITx Pro (grades/course-run API consumer)** | REST (edx-api-client) | daily cron | grades + course-run data | `courses/tasks.py` |
| **MicroMasters (batch enrollment/cert/grade refresh)** | REST OAuth2 (edx-api-client) | Fridays every 6h | enrollments, certificates, current grades | `dashboard/tasks.py` |
| **ODL Video Service (edxval registration)** | REST (JWT) | on sync action | video metadata (HLS/MP4 URLs) | `cloudsync/tasks.py` |
